label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to work through this. The question is about the three different jars of low fat milk mentioned in the passage. The task is to figure out which of the four options correctly completes the sentence "There are three different jars of low fat milk, and ___." First, I'll revisit the part of the passage that talks about these jars. The relevant section says: "One store has three different jars of low fat milk. One says '1 percent fat' on the jar. The second says '99 percent fat free'. The third says 'LOW FAT' in big letters and '1%' in small letters. As you can see, all the milk has the same amount of fat. The milk is all the same. However, in this store the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money. Maybe the customers will buy the milk that costs the most." Now I need to analyze the options based on this information. Option A: one has more fat than the other two. But the passage explicitly states "all the milk has the same amount of fat." So A is incorrect. Option B: they all cost the same amount of money. The passage says the opposite: "the three jars of milk cost three different amounts of money." So B is also incorrect.
